author: cartographer
title: Native Tool System — Complete Architectural Map

Tool_Metadata.purpose: Generic metadata bag carried in ExecuteResult

Tool_DynamicDescription.purpose: Lazy tool description that can depend on the current agent

definition.execute: (args: Schema.Schema.Type<Parameters>, ctx: Context) => Effect.Effect<ExecuteResult<M>, never, R>

Tool_Def.purpose: Fully resolved tool definition — has an id, positioned for direct use

Tool_DefWithoutID.purpose: Tool definition without id — used inside the define() init block; id is added by define()

Tool_Info.purpose: Lazy tool descriptor — the result of Tool.define(). init() is a lazy wrapper that compiles the parser and wires caching/truncation

Tool_Context.purpose: Execution context passed to every tool's execute() function

Tool_ExecuteResult.purpose: Standard return type for all tool execute functions

InvalidArgumentsError.purpose: Raised when LLM calls a tool with invalid args; produces model-facing prose

Tool_define.signature: Tool.define<Parameters, Result, R, ID extends string>(id: ID, init: Effect.Effect<any, any, R>): Effect.Effect<Info<Parameters, Result>, never, R | Truncate.Service | Agent.Service> & { id: ID }
how_it_works: 1. Takes a tool ID string and an Effect that produces a DefWithoutID
2. Inside, yields Truncate.Service, Agent.Service, and ToolCache.Service
3. Returns an Info object with { id, init: wrap(id, resolved, truncate, agents, cache) }
4. The returned Effect has an .id property attached via Object.assign for static reference

Tool_wrap.signature: wrap(id, init, truncate, agents, cache) => () => Effect<DefWithoutID>
what_it_does: 1. Compiles the Schema parser once (closure hoisting)
2. Wraps the execute function to add:
a. Schema decoding with InvalidArgumentsError on failure
b. Optional caching via ToolCache.getOrCompute when cacheable=true
3. Wraps everything in Effect.orDie + Effect.withSpan for OTEL tracing

Tool_init.signature: Tool.init<P, M, R>(info: Info<P, M, R>): Effect.Effect<Def<P, M, R>>
Tool_init.purpose: Resolves an Info into a fully initialized Def by calling info.init()

Tool_InferDef.definition: type InferDef<T> = T extends Info<infer P, infer M> ? Def<P, M> : T extends Effect.Effect<Info<infer P, infer M>> ? Def<P, M> : never
Tool_InferDef.purpose: Extracts the Def type from a tool Info or Effect<Info>

state.custom: Tool.Def[] — dynamically loaded from config dirs and plugins
state.builtin: Tool.Def[] — all statically registered built-in tools
state.task: TaskDef — the special task agent dispatch tool

registration_flow.stateless_init: Each tool is imported (e.g., import { GrepTool } from './grep') and yielded from Effect.gen() to resolve its Info
registration_flow.Tool_init_call: yield* Tool.init(yield* GrepTool) produces the fully resolved Def (with compiled parser, caching, truncation)
registration_flow.Effect_all: All tools are initialized in a single Effect.all block (concurrent yielding)
registration_flow.filtering: Tools are filtered by runtime flags: experimentalScout, experimentalLspTool, experimentalPlanMode, experimentalCoordination, experimentalDuckDBQuery, enableQuestionTool, client type

model_based_filtering.ApplyPatchTool: enabled for non-oss gpt models (excluding gpt-4)

plugin_tools.how: fromPlugin(id, def) wraps plugin ToolDefinition into Tool.Def
plugin_tools.args_conversion: Zod args → JSON Schema7 with runtime normalization
plugin_tools.plugin_context: Bridges Effect-based ctx.ask() to Promise-based via EffectBridge

always: Tool.define() with an id string (kebab-case, like 'smart_edit')
An Effect.gen init block that yields services then returns DefWithoutID
description from a .txt file (convention, not required)
parameters as Schema.Struct with annotated descriptions
execute function that returns ExecuteResult with title, metadata, output
export const XxxTool as the named export
sometimes: cacheable: true — for pure tools (grep, read-source, etc.)
Permission ask (ctx.ask) — for tools that need confirmation

ai_sdk_bridge.overview: Native tools are converted to AI SDK Tool objects in session/tools.ts

conversion.for_each_tool: for (const item of yield* registry.tools({ modelID, providerID, agent }))
conversion.description: Appends dynamic task/skill descriptions to the static tool description
conversion.ai_tool_creation: tools[item.id] = tool({ description: ..., inputSchema: jsonSchema(schema), execute(args, options) { ... } })

execution_lifecycle_in_wrapper: 1. ctx = context(args, options) — builds Tool.Context from AI SDK ToolExecutionOptions
3. Execution.publishStarted — bus event for coordination system
4. Effect.catch — wraps item.execute() to catch errors
5. Execution.publishCompleted or publishFailed — bus event on result/error
6. output.attachments — adds PartID to each attachment
ai_sdk_bridge.mcp_side: MCP tools also flow into the same tools dict, with their own permission/truncation wrapper

tool_flow: 1. SessionPrompt.prompt() calls SessionTools.resolve() to get Record<string, AITool>
2. The tools are passed into LLMRequestPrep.prepare()
3. prepare() filters tools by agent permissions + user overrides via resolveTools()
4. Prepared tools go to LLMService.stream() as part of StreamInput.tools
5. LLM.stream() calls streamText() from the 'ai' npm package with the tools dict
6. streamText() handles tool dispatch automatically — calls execute() on each tool
llm_integration.model_filtering: ProviderTransform.schema() adapts JSON Schema per-model (some models need specific schema formats)

ToolJsonSchema.fromSchema: Converts Effect Schema.Top to JSONSchema7 via Schema.toJsonSchemaDocument
ToolJsonSchema.fromTool: Returns pre-computed jsonSchema or falls back to fromSchema
ToolJsonSchema.normalization: Strips null, flattens anyOf/allOf, bounds integers, inlines $ref

Execution.purpose: Publishes coordination bus events for tool lifecycle
Execution.consumer: Coordination system, UI, analytics, session recording

Truncate.purpose: Agent-aware output truncation for tool results

InvalidArgumentsError.purpose: Typed error raised when schema validation fails — message getter produces model-facing prose for the AI SDK rewrite loop

native_vs_non_native_distinction.native_definition: A tool is 'native' if it's defined via Tool.define() in a file under packages/opencode/src/tool/ and imported+registered in registry.ts
key_distinctions: Native tools are Effect-first: parameters are Effect Schema, execute returns Effect<ExecuteResult>
Native tools have lazy initialization: define() returns an Effect<Info>, not a ready Def; Info.init() is called lazily
Native tools are wired into Effect DI: they yield* services (Truncate, Agent, ToolCache, domain services)
Non-native tools come from: plugin system (Zod args, Promise-based execute) or MCP (AI SDK tool format)
Plugin tools are wrapped at the registry boundary via fromPlugin() — bridge to native format
MCP tools flow alongside native in SessionTools.resolve() but keep their AI SDK execute shape

steps: │   const greptool = yield* GrepTool   // resolves Info    │
│   const grep = yield* Tool.init(greptool) // calls init  │
│           TOOL EXECUTION (when LLM calls a tool)         │

naming.file_name: kebab-case matching tool_id with underscores (e.g., smart-edit.ts → tool_id 'smart_edit')

exports.namespace_re_export: export * as Xxx from './xxx' (used by ~37 of 59 tool files)
exports.no_default: No tool file uses default export

conventions.description: Pulled from .txt files in the same directory (e.g., grep.txt, read-source.txt)
conventions.parameters: Schema.Struct with Schema.String.annotate({ description: '...' }) for LLM-facing descriptions
conventions.error_handling: Execute always ends with .pipe(Effect.orDie) — fatal errors crash the Effect runtime intentionally
conventions.dependencies: Yielded in the init Effect, not the execute closure — init runs once, execute runs per-call

smoking_guns.finding: Tool caching has no TTL — cache entries live until explicit invalidate()
smoking_guns.note: storedAt field is recorded but never read

smoking_guns.finding: Cache key is naive: `${id}:${JSON.stringify(decoded)}` — JSON.stringify of same args may differ by key ordering
execution.ts is untracked (new file) and uses 'any' for the bus publish return type
smoking_guns.note: No interface or typed payload model
